phases: Phase 2: previous_hash uniqueness detection at seal_entry_check
Phase 3: SKILL.md prose + SG doctrine entry

limitations: V1 forward-only; no renumber of past entries; SHA256[:12] truncation; previous_hash uniqueness at seal_entry_check (single-session).
non_goals: No replacement of Entry #N as primary identifier
exclusions: No changes to chain_hash/content_hash/Merkle semantics; no changes to past Entries #1-#207; no new substantiate steps.

impact_assessment.purpose: META_LEDGER concurrent-federation safety via forward-only content-addressable IDs + previous_hash uniqueness detection.
affected_stakeholders: Federation operators running concurrent SDLC cycles
Downstream auditors reading sealed META_LEDGER entries
Tools parsing the ledger (seal_entry_check, ledger_hash.verify, badge_currency)
identified_risks: Forward-only design leaves past duplicates (#16a/b, #17a/b, #18a/b) unfixed (documented residual)
previous_hash uniqueness false-positive on fork-merge patterns
Schema extension breaks existing META_LEDGER parsers
mitigations: 48-bit space + opt-in 64-char full-hash via env var
V1 explicitly defers retroactive renumber; SG doctrine forbids history-rewrite
Uniqueness check single-session at substantiate-time only
Additive field; existing Entry #N format preserved verbatim
Helper signature anchored with regression tests
residual_risks: Past duplicate entries remain in the ledger; pending V2 operator-authorized reconciliation
